Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows significant renal impairment (elevated creatinine and urea), marked proteinuria, glucosuria, and ketonuria, with low urine specific gravity and acidic pH, suggesting possible nephrotic syndrome or diabetic nephropathy, though HbA1c is unexpectedly low. Urine is cloudy and brown, but no evidence of infection. Further nephrological evaluation and assessment for underlying metabolic or systemic disease are recommended.
